title: Scratches Are Too Deep
description: Visible sanding scratches, gouges, or marks remain after sanding.

title: 320 Grit Is Not Removing Scratches
description: 320 grit is too fine to remove deeper scratches left by 180 grit or heavy pressure.

title: Sandpaper Clogs Too Fast
description: Dust, paint, finish, or residue loads into the abrasive and stops cutting.

title: Paint Clogs Sandpaper
description: Soft paint or finish residue smears into the sheet during sanding.

title: Surface Still Feels Rough
description: The surface does not become smooth even after sanding.

title: Wet Sanding Leaves Haze
description: Wet sanding leaves a cloudy, dull, or hazy surface.
customer_phrases: dull clear coat after wet sanding

title: Wrong Grit Progression
description: Skipped grit steps can leave old scratches behind.

title: Not Sure What Grit To Use
description: Find a starting grit or the next grit based on the sanding stage.
customer_phrases: not sure what grit to use

title: What Grit For Wood
description: Choose a grit based on whether wood needs removal, prep, or finish sanding.

title: What Grit For Metal
description: Use coarser grits for oxidation or scratch removal and finer grits for prep.

title: Sanding Between Coats
description: Use light pressure and fine grits so the next coat bonds without deep scratches.

title: Poor Results Between Coats
description: Intercoat sanding leaves scratches, cut-through, or a rough surface.

title: Swirl Marks Remain
description: Circular or curved sanding marks remain visible.

title: Sandpaper Tears Early
description: The sheet rips, catches, or wears through during use.

title: Paint Removal Is Slow
description: A grit may be too fine or the sheet may be loaded or worn.

title: Sanding Takes Too Long
description: The abrasive is cutting slowly or the task feels inefficient.

title: Finish Looks Uneven
description: Patchy sanding marks, dull spots, or inconsistent texture remain.

title: Clear Coat Wet Sanding
description: Fine wet sanding can level defects before polishing when the coating is ready.

title: eQualle Assorted Sandpaper Kit 80-3000
description: Support guide for the eQualle assorted 9 x 11 inch silicon carbide sandpaper sheets kit, 80 through 3000 grit, for wet or dry use.

title: Grit comes off the sheet

title: Sheet curls during wet sanding
description: The sheet curls up and becomes hard to keep flat while wet sanding.

title: Sheet becomes soft after soaking too long
description: The sheet feels weak or soft after sitting in water.

description: The edge of the sandpaper frays and leaves fibers or rough backing.

description: Small details are difficult to sand evenly with a loose sheet.

title: How to use 80 to 3000 grit sandpaper
description: Choose lower grits for removal, middle grits for preparation, and 1000 through 3000 for wet sanding or polishing preparation.
customer_phrases: how to use 80 to 3000 grit sandpaper

title: What grit should I start with in the assorted kit
description: Start with the least aggressive grit that still removes the defect, then refine through smaller grit steps.
customer_phrases: what grit should I start with in assorted kit
which grit should I start with

title: 3000 grit does not make gloss
description: 3000 grit leaves a very fine sanding haze and does not create gloss by itself.

title: Which sheet from the kit should I use
description: Pick the sheet by surface, defect depth, and sanding stage: removal, prep, fine finishing, or wet sanding.
